name: dev.ucp.common.identity_linking
title: Identity Linking
description: Capability schema for identity linking. Businesses declare which capabilities offer user-scoped features, and whether user identity is required for each, in the capability config.
$comment: Auth mechanism: uses business-hosted OAuth 2.0 with RFC 8414 discovery. The 'config' object is open for non-breaking extension. Reserved extension points: 'providers' (map of trusted identity providers keyed by reverse-domain, with a 'type' discriminator defaulting to 'oauth2' — enabling delegated IdP, identity chaining, and future non-OAuth mechanisms such as wallet attestation). When 'providers' is absent, platforms MUST use OAuth 2.0 with RFC 8414 discovery on the business domain. Platforms MUST ignore unrecognized fields in 'config' and fall back to this default behavior.

capability_identity_config.title: Capability Identity Configuration
capability_identity_config.description: Per-capability identity configuration. Declares whether user authentication is required and what sub-scopes govern user-authenticated access. When 'scopes' is absent, the capability name itself is the scope token (coarse-grained). When 'scopes' is present, each entry is joined to the capability name with a colon on the wire (e.g., 'dev.ucp.shopping.order:read').

auth_required.description: Whether this capability requires user identity. When true, the business returns an 'identity_required' error for requests that arrive without a user identity token. When false (default), the capability operates at agent-authenticated access level and user identity upgrades the experience (e.g., enabling personalized pricing or saved addresses).

scopes.description: Sub-scope identifiers within the capability, defined by the capability's own spec as named operation groups (e.g., 'read', 'manage'). Each entry is joined to the capability name with a colon to form the wire-format scope token. When absent, the capability name alone is the scope.

platform_schema.title: Identity Linking (Platform)
platform_schema.description: Platform-level identity linking capability declaration. Platforms advertise support for identity linking; no auth-specific config is required.

business_schema.title: Identity Linking (Business)
business_schema.description: Business-level identity linking configuration. Businesses declare which capabilities offer user-scoped features and whether user identity is required for each.

config.$comment: Reserved extension point: 'providers' (map of trusted identity providers keyed by reverse-domain). See schema-level $comment for details.

capabilities.description: Map of capabilities that offer user-scoped features, keyed by capability name (reverse-domain format). Capabilities absent from this map have no user-scoped features — they operate at public or agent-authenticated access only. Platforms derive the authorization scope set by intersecting this map with the negotiated capability set.
